content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA

POLITICAL REFORM AUDIT PROGRAM
1601 C STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 322-2973

Dec 27 4:00PM '78

December 27, 1978


W. A. McCormick
Post Office Box 1414
Glendale, CA 91209

A 78-353


Thank you for your letter of December 21 regarding our audit reports.
These reports are public documents and are sent by this office to the
Fair Political Practices Commission and the Attorney General as
required by Section 90004 of the Political Reform Act. In addition,
copies are sent to the filers and to the Office of the Secretary of
State where public files are maintained.

We have not, to this date, received requests for copies of these reports
from any other source. In the absence of requests and in the interest
of keeping down program costs, we do not intend to expand the distribution
of the reports.


Robert C. Summers
Chief, Political Reform Audit Division

cc: Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

from the desk of:

W. A. "BILL" McCORMICK 12/21/78

Gentlemen,

Inasmuch as copies of campaign reports must be
filed with the county clerk or registrar re-
corder of the principal county in which the
committee is located, as well as the secretary
of state's office, why are the audit reports
pertaining to those same committees not made
available to the appropriate county offices as
well? Would not this increase consistency?

Sincerely,

W. A. McCormick
Post Office Box 1414
Glendale, Ca., 91209

CC: FPPC, Sacramento
